zotero.abstractNote: Reinforcement learning (RL) has shown impressive results in simulation, but transferring these policies to real-world robots remains challenging. A major bottleneck is the sample efficiency of RL algorithms, especially when the reward signal is sparse, such as binary success/failure signals. In this paper, we propose a high acceleration reinforcement learning approach that combines a model-based trajectory optimizer with a model-free policy search. We demonstrate that our approach can learn to juggle a ball with a real-world robot arm using only binary rewards. Our method achieves this by using the trajectory optimizer to generate high-quality demonstrations, which are then used to initialize the policy, and by using the model-free policy search to refine the policy in the real world. We show that our approach is significantly more sample-efficient than state-of-the-art model-free RL algorithms and can learn to juggle in a few hundred trials.

extra.Editors: Jens Kober and Fabio Ramos and Claire J. Tomlin

zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 4th Conference on Robot Learning
